notes: Targeting WINs with fewest open issues (WIN-056: 1 issue, WIN-058: 1 issue - both DOCX-only, not wins.json patchable). Three wins.json patches this run: CHAMP URL fix (WIN-039), CUORE citation removal (WIN-015), and code_analysis sync preparation for WIN-032/033/065 via review file updates. apply-patches.js is ABSENT from build-scripts/ - patches require manual application.

patches.find: Replication attempts explain results via classical near-field coupling. CUORE experiment shows expected Faraday attenuation.
patches.replace: Replication attempts explain results via classical near-field coupling.
patches.rationale: FACTUAL ERROR + BROKEN LINK: CUORE (Cryogenic Underground Observatory for Rare Events, LNGS) is a neutrinoless double beta decay experiment using TeO2 bolometers — it has no connection to Faraday rotation or attenuation. Citing CUORE for 'expected Faraday attenuation' is a category error that any dome defender could exploit to impugn the review's credibility. The URL https://cuore.lngs.infn.it/ also returns HTTP 404 (confirmed by integrity report 2026-04-06). Removing the incorrect sentence leaves the preceding sentence intact and eliminates the vulnerability.

BROKEN LINK: https://op.gfz-potsdam.de/champ/ returns HTTP 404 (confirmed by integrity report 2026-04-06). GFZ reorganized its web infrastructure; the canonical URL for the CHAMP satellite mission is now https://www.gfz.de/en/section/geomagnetism/infrastructure/gfz-satellite-mission-champ (already in use at WIN-029, verified working). Consistent citation hygiene is essential given we criticize the dome model for misrepresenting sources.

code_analysis_sync.description: ISS-048: code_analysis tags added to curmudgeon review files for WIN-032, WIN-033, WIN-065 during this run. Apply with: node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace

WIN-032.rationale: New Jerusalem pole axis is a purely theological/mythological claim with no code validation whatsoever — dome_code_analysis confirms no script references WIN-032. Not relabeling standard physics (it predates physics). Post-hoc because the assertion is a static text string, not a derived prediction.

WIN-033.rationale: Sigma Octantis dimness uses 'aetheric depth' language to explain differential star brightness — this relabels standard atmospheric extinction without adding numeric content. No code found in dome repository for this WIN. No n(r)-based formula predicts the specific brightness difference.

WIN-065.rationale: Polaris systematic excess relabels celestial navigation (latitude = Polaris altitude, known to arcsecond precision) as dome evidence. The +0.27 degree figure is within combined error budget (±0.3–0.5 degree). No monitoring script in dome repository. Prediction may predate claimed registration date given V12 coordinate history.
